# SSHGate classifier corpus.
# Format: <EXPECTED>\t<cmd>
# EXPECTED: READ or WRITE. Comment lines (#) and blank lines are ignored.
# Sourced verbatim from spec §"Command Classification".

# --- Read list (auto-allowed) ---
READ	cat /etc/hosts
READ	less /var/log/syslog
READ	head -n 50 /etc/passwd
READ	tail -f /var/log/nginx/access.log
READ	wc -l /etc/services
READ	file /usr/bin/ls
READ	stat /etc/hosts
READ	readlink /usr/bin/python
READ	ls -la /var/log
READ	find /var/log -name "*.gz"
READ	locate nginx.conf
READ	which docker
READ	whereis ssh
READ	grep -r "error" /var/log
READ	awk '{print $1}' /etc/passwd
READ	sed -n '1,10p' /etc/hosts
READ	sort /etc/passwd
READ	uniq /tmp/list
READ	diff a.txt b.txt
READ	df -h
READ	du -sh /var/log
READ	free -m
READ	uptime
READ	uname -a
READ	hostname
READ	whoami
READ	id
READ	groups testuser
READ	ps aux
READ	top -b -n 1
READ	pgrep -f nginx
READ	lsof -i :80
READ	ss -tulpn
READ	netstat -an
READ	ip addr
READ	ping -c 3 8.8.8.8
READ	dig example.com
READ	nslookup example.com
READ	traceroute google.com
READ	curl https://example.com
READ	curl -s https://api.example.com/v1/status
READ	wget -q -O- https://example.com
READ	journalctl -u nginx
READ	dmesg
READ	last
READ	w
READ	who
READ	systemctl status nginx
READ	systemctl is-active nginx
READ	systemctl is-enabled nginx
READ	systemctl list-units --type=service
READ	docker ps
READ	docker logs my-container
READ	docker inspect my-container
READ	docker images
READ	docker stats --no-stream
READ	git status
READ	git log --oneline
READ	git diff HEAD~1
READ	git show HEAD
READ	git branch -a
READ	date
READ	timedatectl
READ	env
READ	printenv PATH
READ	cat /proc/cpuinfo
READ	cat /proc/meminfo
READ	cat /sys/class/net/eth0/address

# --- Write list (signature required) ---
WRITE	rm /tmp/foo
WRITE	rm -rf /var/cache/foo
WRITE	mv /tmp/a /tmp/b
WRITE	cp /etc/hosts /tmp/hosts.bak
WRITE	mkdir -p /opt/app
WRITE	rmdir /tmp/empty
WRITE	touch /tmp/marker
WRITE	chmod 0644 /etc/hosts
WRITE	chown root:root /etc/hosts
WRITE	chgrp wheel /var/log
WRITE	sed -i 's/foo/bar/g' /etc/hosts
WRITE	find /tmp -name "*.log" -delete
WRITE	find /tmp -name "*.log" -exec rm {} \;
WRITE	systemctl start nginx
WRITE	systemctl stop nginx
WRITE	systemctl restart nginx
WRITE	systemctl enable nginx
WRITE	systemctl disable nginx
WRITE	service nginx restart
WRITE	apt install -y nginx
WRITE	apt-get update
WRITE	yum install -y httpd
WRITE	dnf upgrade -y
WRITE	pacman -Syu
WRITE	pip install requests
WRITE	npm install express
WRITE	docker run -d nginx
WRITE	docker stop my-container
WRITE	docker rm my-container
WRITE	docker exec my-container sh
WRITE	git commit -m "msg"
WRITE	git push origin main
WRITE	git checkout main
WRITE	git merge feature
WRITE	git reset --hard HEAD
WRITE	kill 1234
WRITE	killall nginx
WRITE	pkill -f python
WRITE	mount /dev/sda1 /mnt
WRITE	umount /mnt
WRITE	iptables -A INPUT -j DROP
WRITE	ufw enable
WRITE	firewall-cmd --reload
WRITE	useradd alice
WRITE	userdel bob
WRITE	usermod -aG wheel alice
WRITE	passwd alice
WRITE	groupadd devs
WRITE	crontab -e
WRITE	at now + 1 minute
WRITE	reboot
WRITE	shutdown -h now
WRITE	halt
WRITE	vi /etc/hosts
WRITE	vim /etc/hosts
WRITE	nano /etc/hosts
WRITE	emacs /etc/hosts

# --- Tricky / classification-rule cases ---
# Pipes: if ANY segment is a write, the whole thing is a write.
WRITE	ps aux | tee /tmp/x
READ	cat /etc/hosts | grep foo
READ	ls -la | head -n 5
WRITE	cat /etc/hosts | tee /tmp/copy
# Redirects (>, >>) → write.
WRITE	echo hi > /tmp/x
WRITE	cat /etc/hosts >> /tmp/dump
READ	cat </dev/zero | head -c 10
# Semicolons / && / ||: any write segment → write.
WRITE	ls; rm -f /tmp/x
READ	ls && echo done
WRITE	ls && rm /tmp/x
WRITE	ls || rm /tmp/x
# sudo prefix → always write, regardless of what follows.
WRITE	sudo cat /etc/hosts
WRITE	sudo ls /root
# curl with -X POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH or -d/--data → write; bare curl → read.
WRITE	curl -X POST https://example.com/api
WRITE	curl -X PUT https://example.com/api
WRITE	curl -X DELETE https://example.com/api
WRITE	curl -X PATCH https://example.com/api
WRITE	curl -d "k=v" https://example.com
WRITE	curl --data "k=v" https://example.com
# Unknown commands default to write.
WRITE	frobnicate --foo
WRITE	madeupbinary arg1 arg2

# --- v1.1 Task B: refined pipe/chain segment classification ---
# All-read pipelines/chains are now READ (v1 used to flag these as WRITE).
READ	cat /etc/hosts | grep foo
READ	ps aux | head -5
READ	ls /tmp | wc -l
READ	journalctl -u nginx | tail -20
READ	df -h ; free -m
READ	uptime && uname -a
READ	cat /tmp/x|grep foo
# Output redirects keep the chain a WRITE even if every command in it is a read.
WRITE	cat /etc/hosts > /tmp/copy
WRITE	echo hi | tee -a /tmp/foo
# Command and process substitution are opaque → fail-safe WRITE.
WRITE	ls $(rm /tmp/x)
WRITE	echo `whoami`
WRITE	diff <(ls /a) <(ls /b)
WRITE	tee >(cat) < /dev/null

# --- v1.1 Task B: tightened git/wget/curl rules (Mi3, Mi4) ---
WRITE	git stash
WRITE	git stash push
WRITE	git stash pop
WRITE	git restore main.go
WRITE	git switch main
WRITE	git config --set user.name alice
READ	git stash list
READ	git stash show stash@{0}
READ	git config --get user.name
WRITE	wget https://example.com/file.tar
READ	wget -O- https://example.com/cfg
READ	wget --output-document=- https://example.com/cfg
WRITE	curl -o /tmp/x https://example.com
WRITE	curl --output /tmp/x https://example.com
READ	curl https://example.com/api/status

# --- 2026-05-19 read-only-gate bypass BLOCKERs (security research audit) ---
# B1: sed `e` flag / `w`/`r`/`R` commands execute shell or write files.
WRITE	sed 's/.*/id/e'
WRITE	sed -e 's/X/Y/e' /etc/hosts
WRITE	sed 's/X/Y/w /tmp/log' file
WRITE	sed '1r /etc/passwd' file
# B1 — simple-reader sed forms still pass.
READ	sed 's/foo/bar/' /etc/hosts

# B2: find `-fprint*`, `-fls`, `-ok*` write files or exec commands.
WRITE	find /tmp -fprintf /etc/passwd 'evil\n'
WRITE	find /tmp -fprint /tmp/list
WRITE	find /tmp -fls /tmp/list
WRITE	find /tmp -fprint0 /tmp/list
WRITE	find /tmp -ok rm {} \;
# B2 — simple find readers still pass.
READ	find /tmp -name '*.log'
READ	find /tmp -name '*.log' -print

# B3: env-var smuggling — dangerous KEY=VAL prefixes force WRITE.
WRITE	GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF='sh -c "id"' git log -p
WRITE	GIT_SSH_COMMAND='ssh -o ProxyCommand=...' git ls-remote origin
WRITE	LD_PRELOAD=/tmp/evil.so cat /etc/hosts
WRITE	IFS=$'\n' ls /etc
WRITE	PATH=/tmp ls
WRITE	PYTHONSTARTUP=/tmp/evil.py python -c "print(1)"

# B4: awk `system()`, `getline`, pipe-to-cmd, redirect-to-file → WRITE.
WRITE	awk 'BEGIN{system("rm /tmp/x")}'
WRITE	awk 'BEGIN{getline x; print x}' /etc/hosts
WRITE	awk '{print | "rm /tmp/x"}' file
WRITE	awk '{print > "/tmp/log"}' file
# B4 — simple awk readers still pass.
READ	awk '{print $1}' /etc/passwd
READ	awk '/^root/ {print $1}' /etc/passwd
